majorEmployers.name: Covercraft Industries
majorEmployers.notes: Founded in 1965, headquarters in Pauls Valley with manufacturing facilities in multiple states

majorEmployers.name: Wynnewood Refining Company (CVR Energy)
majorEmployers.notes: Petroleum refinery producing gasoline, propane, fuel oils and solvents

majorEmployers.name: Pauls Valley Public Schools
majorEmployers.notes: Largest school district in Garvin County serving Pauls Valley area

majorEmployers.name: Lindsay Public Schools
majorEmployers.notes: Serves approximately 1,186 students across 3 schools in northwestern Garvin County

majorEmployers.name: Wynnewood Public Schools
majorEmployers.notes: Serves approximately 688 students across 3 schools in southern Garvin County

majorEmployers.name: Garvin County Community Living Center
majorEmployers.notes: Non-profit providing residential and vocational services for individuals with developmental disabilities since 1982

uniqueFacts: Lindsay was known as the 'Broomcorn Capital of the World' — between 1915 and 1946, Oklahoma led the nation in broomcorn production, and the Lindsay area harvested more broomcorn than any other region globally by 1961
Famed aviator Wiley Post, the first person to fly solo around the world (1933) and discoverer of the jet stream, grew up on a farm near Maysville in Garvin County
The county slogan 'We Sweep the World' was adopted due to its dominance in broomcorn production
The Toy and Action Figure Museum in Pauls Valley was named one of Time Magazine's 'Top 50 Most Authentic American Experiences' and draws visitors from across the country

The Initial Point, from which all of Oklahoma except the Panhandle was surveyed, is located on the boundary between Garvin and Murray counties and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places
processServingNotes: Returns of service for Garvin County District Court are filed with the Court Clerk at the courthouse located at 201 W. Grant Ave in Pauls Valley, open Monday through Friday 8:30am to 4:30pm

Garvin County is served by eight local law enforcement agencies including Pauls Valley Police, Lindsay Police, Wynnewood Police, and Garvin County Sheriff, each with separate jurisdictions for attempting service
The county spans approximately 814 square miles across south-central Oklahoma, with significant rural ranch and oil-field worker housing that may require GPS and skip-trace tools to locate
heroLead: Garvin County lies in the heart of south-central Oklahoma's Red Bed Plains and Sandstone Hills, where the Washita River winds through rolling ranchland that once produced enough broomcorn to earn Lindsay the title 'Broomcorn Capital of the World.' From the historic Santa Fe Depot in Pauls Valley to the oil refineries of Wynnewood, our process servers know every road in this 814-square-mile county. Whether your papers need to reach a defendant in town or at a rural ranch gate, call (539) 367-6832 or visit /contact to get started.

faqs.question: Where is the Garvin County Courthouse located and what are its hours?
faqs.answer: The Garvin County Courthouse is located at 201 W. Grant Ave in Pauls Valley, Oklahoma 73075. The Court Clerk's office is open Monday through Friday from 8:30am to 4:30pm. All district court filings, including returns of service, are handled at this location. For process serving inquiries in Garvin County, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: Which judicial district serves Garvin County?
faqs.answer: Garvin County is part of Oklahoma's 21st Judicial District, which also includes Cleveland and McClain counties. The District Judge presiding over Garvin County District Court is Hon. Judge Candace L. Blalock. The District Attorney's office is located in the courthouse on the corner of Grant and Walnut streets in Pauls Valley.

faqs.question: What is the geographic coverage area for process serving in Garvin County?
faqs.answer: Garvin County covers approximately 814 square miles in south-central Oklahoma, stretching from the I-35 corridor through Pauls Valley and Wynnewood to the rural ranchlands west of the Washita River. Our process servers cover all incorporated communities including Pauls Valley, Lindsay, Wynnewood, Elmore City, Stratford, Maysville, Paoli, Katie, Foster, and Erin Springs, as well as all rural routes and oil-field worker housing.

faqs.question: What tribal jurisdiction applies in Garvin County?
faqs.answer: The entirety of Garvin County lies within the Chickasaw Nation treaty territory. The Chickasaw Nation Lighthorse Police Department has jurisdiction across the county, and tribal citizens may be enrolled members. Process servers should be aware that certain legal matters involving tribal members may have specific jurisdictional considerations under federal and tribal law.

faqs.question: How long does process serving typically take in Garvin County?
faqs.answer: Standard service in Pauls Valley, Lindsay, and Wynnewood typically completes within 2 to 5 business days due to I-35 corridor access. Rural addresses near Stratford, Elmore City, or Maysville may add a day depending on server schedule. Rush and same-day service are available for time-sensitive matters in the Pauls Valley area. Contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832 for a specific quote based on your timeline.

faqs.question: What are the largest communities and employment centers in Garvin County?
faqs.answer: Pauls Valley (population approximately 6,100) is the county seat and largest city, home to the courthouse, Covercraft Industries, and the Santa Fe Depot Museum. Lindsay is known for its historic broomcorn industry, and Wynnewood hosts a major oil refinery. The county's economy is rooted in agriculture, oil production, and manufacturing. Just Legal Solutions provides process serving coverage to all communities in Garvin County.
